An imposing array of Great North
ern railroad officials visited Spokane
today. including two of President J.
]. Hills sons, l.. w. Hill and Walter
Hill. The others in the party were
f. e. Ward. general manager: George
T. Slade, general superintendent: Mr..
Patterson, assistant chief engineer
Mr.. Blabon, fourth vice president
Howard James. purchasing agent: ].
A. Miller, general agent OF Seattle,
and Assistant General Superintendent
ii. A. Kennedy. They were travelling
by special train composed of two
baggage and three private cars. The
officials inspected the shops at Hin-
yard this morning and later ran down
to the city and were sidetracked al
the depot for an hour General Su-
perintendent Slade stated that there
was no special significance attached
to the trip.


I'VE are just making an examina-
ton of our property-just an inspec-
tion trip, you know. No, there Is
nothing doing of special interest to
Spokane"


He inquired about the war news
and the last reports from the fire
and was handed q Press extra with
the latest.


The special left at 1890 p. m. 70,
Seattle.


Mr.. II. r. Braden, the smelter mag
nate Of Helena, accompanied by his
wife. was registered at the Spokane

join the Hin party on their western
trip.
